# asim_parsers.csv

**Generated by:** [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.py`](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md)
**Granularity:** One row per ASIM parser (union and source parsers from `/Parsers/ASim*/Parsers`)
**Type:** Entity table

## Overview

All ASIM (Advanced Security Information Model) parsers, with full schema metadata: schema name and version, parser type (union / source / empty), product/vendor, sub-parsers (for union parsers), parameter signatures, and computed connector associations.

## Use Cases

- **ASIM parser inventory** — group by `schema` to see all parsers per ASIM schema (Authentication, Dns, NetworkSession, etc.).
- **Source-parser → product mapping** — `product_name` identifies the product each source parser normalizes.
- **Union-parser composition** — `sub_parsers` lists the source parsers a union parser aggregates.
- **Connector association** — `associated_connectors` and `associated_solutions` show which connectors provide data to each parser (computed via shared tables + filter-field subset matching).
- **Selection-criteria analysis** — `filter_fields` shows what the parser filters on (e.g., `CommonSecurityLog.DeviceVendor == "Acme"`).
- **ASIM Schema Browser** — consumed by [`generate_asim_browser.py`](../generate_asim_browser.md).
- **Kusto upload** — uploaded as `solution_analyzer_asim_parsers_lookup` by `upload_to_kusto.py`.

## Columns

| Column | Description | Data Source |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| `parser_name` | Parser function name (e.g., `ASimDnsAzureFirewall`) | YAML `ParserName` |
| `equivalent_builtin` | Built-in parser alias (e.g., `_ASim_Dns_AzureFirewall`) | YAML `EquivalentBuiltInParser` |
| `schema` | ASIM schema name (e.g., `Dns`, `NetworkSession`, `Authentication`) | Inferred from folder name (`ASim{Schema}`) |
| `schema_version` | Schema version number | YAML `Normalization.Version` |
| `parser_type` | `union` (schema-level aggregator), `source` (product-specific), or `empty` (placeholder) | Computed |
| `parser_title` | Display title | YAML `Parser.Title` |
| `parser_version` | Parser version number | YAML `Parser.Version` |
| `parser_last_updated` | Last update date | YAML `Parser.LastUpdated` |
| `product_name` | Product/source name (e.g., `Azure Firewall`, `Palo Alto`) | YAML `Normalization.Product` |
| `description` | Parser description | YAML `Description` |
| `tables` | Semicolon-separated source tables | KQL parsing of `ParserQuery` |
| `sub_parsers` | Semicolon-separated sub-parser references (for union parsers) | YAML `Parsers` array |
| `parser_params` | Parser parameters in `name:type=default` format | YAML `ParserParams` array |
| `filter_fields` | Pipe-separated `Table.Field op "value"` clauses extracted from the parser query. Predicates on raw documented columns are attributed to the owning table; predicates on fields defined via `\| extend` use the synthetic table `_Computed`. | See [`map_solutions_connectors_tables.md` › Filter Fields Detection](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md#filter-fields-detection) |
| `associated_connectors` | Connector IDs that provide data matching this parser | [Connector Association Algorithm](../map_solutions_connectors_tables.md#connector-association-algorithm) |
| `associated_solutions` | Solutions of the associated connectors | Derived |
| `references` | Semicolon-separated reference links | YAML `References` array |
| `source_file` | Relative path to the YAML source | File system |
| `github_url` | GitHub URL | Computed |

> ASIM parsers are loaded from YAML files under `/Parsers/ASim*/Parsers`. Union parsers typically have empty `tables` but populated `sub_parsers`. The `vim*` (vendor-independent model) wrappers around `ASim*` parsers are skipped because they have identical filters.

## Related CSVs

- [`asim_fields.csv`](asim_fields.md) — ASIM schema field definitions (consumed alongside this file by the ASIM browser).
- [`asim_parsers_unmatched_report.csv`](asim_parsers_unmatched_report.md) — diagnostic report of ASIM parsers that could not be associated with any connector.
- [`parsers.csv`](parsers.md) — non-ASIM parsers.
- [`connectors.csv`](connectors.md) — for joining `associated_connectors`.
